L1.area: Database driver layer for relational database connections

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied parameters used to construct a database sequence name are not properly escaped, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary SQL statements when the application connects to a PostgreSQL database.

L3.area: Database driver layer for relational database connections
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied parameters used to construct a database sequence name are not properly escaped, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary SQL statements when the application connects to a PostgreSQL database.

ground_truth.reason: The pg_insert_id() method directly concatenates user-supplied table and field name parameters into a SQL query without proper escaping, enabling SQL injection.
